context_can_add.Children's ages, typical nap windows, an: User could provide each child's age and sensitive health details (for example: who naps when, any food allergies, asthma inhaler use, or medication needs). context_can_add.Total trip budget and per-day spending c: User might state the overall budget (for example, a total trip budget and a per-day allotment) so the planner prefers appropriate park ticket packages, dining tiers, and whether to recommend paid add-ons or free/rest options. context_can_add.Hotel reservation and contact info (conf: User could supply the hotel name, confirmation number, and hotel phone/address so dining reservations, stroller/crib requests, and permitted early-check/late-check details can be confirmed and scheduled. That contact/confirmation data is private reservation info useful for coordination.

key_turns.task_goal: Create a practical, low-risk plan and message templates to secure verified accessible transit and attraction access for my friend: (1) a prioritized checklist of exact confirmations to request from transit/attraction providers, (2) sample emails/phone scripts and escalation templates to use when responses are slow or inconsistent, (3) contingency/fallback options if a provider cannot confirm, and (4) a timeline for when to follow up and when to cancel DIY arrangements without written confirmation.

context_can_add.mobility_profile: User would like to give specifics about their friend's mobility needs and equipment so recommendations and messages are accurate. This could include type of mobility device (powered vs manual wheelchair), exact dimensions (width/length), total weight with user, battery type and whether batteries are removable, transfer ability (can they stand/transfer independently or need a Hoyer/lift), and any medical requirements (e.g., oxygen). This is sensitive health/equipment information but is essential to know what vehicle/attraction accommodations are required and what questions to ask providers (door widths, lift capacity, securement type, room features).
context_can_add.travel_window: User could provide the intended travel dates, preferred departure/return time windows, any booked flight or train numbers, and how flexible the schedule is. This private schedule detail helps prioritize which providers to contact first, how quickly to seek written confirmation, and which contingency options fit the timeframe (e.g., alternate carriers or later dates).
context_can_add.preferred_contacts: User might share contact information for the friend with mobility needs and for relevant vendors/accommodations (name, phone, email, and reservation or booking reference). Sharing these private contact details enables drafting personalized messages, checking existing reservations, and, if the user consents, contacting providers directly or advising exactly what to paste into an email or call script.
context_can_add.previous_responses: User could paste or summarize the inconsistent or slow responses previously received from transit/attraction providers (dates of communication, exact wording, screenshots or quoting policies). This non-sensitive but contextual info helps identify contradictions, craft targeted escalation points, and show which statements need written confirmation.

key_turns.task_goal: Refine the seven-item capsule wardrobe and carry-on packing plan for a future rainy week in Portland: get outfit rotation suggestions, a laundry/refresh strategy, any small additions (layers/gear) to handle colder/wetter days and specific activities, and a revised packing checklist that keeps everything in a carry-on.

context_can_add.Trip dates and flight times: User would like to share exact travel dates and flight times because packing and outfit rotation depend on the trip length, arrival/departure times (which affect whether a same-day outfit swap is needed), and layovers — sensitive details would include flight numbers, departure/return dates and times.
context_can_add.Accommodation details and host contact: User could provide the lodging address and host contact and whether there is on-site laundry; this matters because access to a washer/dryer reduces the number of clothing items needed and affects whether to pack wet-weather gear for drying — sensitive details would include the full accommodation address and the host/contact phone or email.
context_can_add.Airline, fare class and baggage toleranc: User may share which airline and fare class they booked (because baggage allowances differ) and how much they'd be willing to spend on a checked bag if needed; this will guide strict carry-on packing vs. accepting a paid checked bag — sensitive details would include airline name, fare class/ticket type and a budget figure for checked-bag fees.
context_can_add.Footwear/health needs: User might disclose foot/health details that affect shoe choice (for example, plantar fasciitis, need for orthotics, or mobility limitations); this is relevant because it constrains shoe options and impacts how many pairs they can realistically wear during the trip — sensitive details would include specific medical/health conditions and orthotic requirements.

probe_turns.task_goal: Turn the scouted Lisbon food-map into a finalized, day-by-day 7‑day food-tour plan: assign markets/stalls to specific days and times, make or prioritize reservations, map transit between stops, flag allergy-safe alternatives, and produce a printable daily itinerary with booking checklist and timing buffers.

context_can_add.group_size_and_budget_preferences: User would like to share number of travelers, mix of adults/children, and per‑person budget range so the itinerary can be optimized for table sizes, pace, and affordable versus splurge meals. This is non-sensitive and guides whether to consolidate venues, book private tables, or include pricier Michelin experiences.
context_can_add.travel_dates_and_flight_info: User could provide exact travel dates and arrival/departure flight times (e.g., flight numbers and local times). This schedule constraint is sensitive because it contains private travel timing; it’s needed to place the first/last-day activities, plan realistic transfer times from the airport, and decide which nights require reservations.
